summary: The agent correctly identifies a terminological error—"Intracellular" should be "**Intercellular**"—referring instead to Intercellular Adhesion Molecules (ICAMs). Despite no explicit source confirming their placement in the immunoglobulin superfamily, the agent infers based on contextual clues such as ligand-receptor dynamics and reference to other Ig-superfamily proteins; these align with well-established biological principles linking ICAM structure/function to the immunoglobulin superfamily.
